text: Robotic Suture Rectopexy for External Full Thickness Rectal Prolapse, a video vignette. Patient is a forty-eight-year-old female with past surgical history of hysterectomy and bladder sling who presented with fetal incontinence. Preoperative workup included endoscopic anal ultrasound, anal manometry, and MRI defecography. Endoscopic anal ultrasound found an intact internal and external anal sphincter, with thinning of each. Anal manometry also reported decreased resting pressure but normal squeeze pressure and normal sensitivity. MRI defecography found severe to moderate tricompartmental descent with the additional findings consisting of a small cystocele, moderate anterior rectocele, and a rectal prolapse with the anal rectal junction descending six point one centimeters below the pubococcygeal line. The patient was evaluated in conjunction with urogynecology, and pelvic floor physical therapy was found to be indicated.

Robotic Suture Rectopexy for External Full Thickness Rectal Prolapse, a video vignette. Patient is a forty-eight-year-old female with past surgical history of hysterectomy and bladder sling who presented with fetal incontinence. Preoperative workup included endoscopic anal ultrasound, anal manometry, and MRI defecography. Endoscopic anal ultrasound found an intact internal and external anal sphincter, with thinning of each. Anal manometry also reported decreased resting pressure but normal squeeze pressure and normal sensitivity. MRI defecography found severe to moderate tricompartmental descent with the additional findings consisting of a small cystocele, moderate anterior rectocele, and a rectal prolapse with the anal rectal junction descending six point one centimeters below the pubococcygeal line. The patient was evaluated in conjunction with urogynecology, and pelvic floor physical therapy was found to be indicated.

The surgeon uses a DaVinci XI robot to perform a robotic suture rectopexy. Four 8mm trocars are placed, and the peritoneum is incised on the right side of the rectum, medial to the right ureter and lateral to the right hypogastric nerve, creating a peritoneal flap for suspension.

The surgeon uses a DaVinci XI robot to perform a combined robotic sacral colpopexy and robotic suture rectopexy. Four 8mm trocars are placed, and the peritoneum is incised on the right side of the rectum, medial to the right ureter and lateral to the right hypogastric nerve, creating a peritoneal flap for suspension. The rectum is mobilized circumferentially, starting posteriorly through the holy plane and progressing towards the pouch of Douglas. The peritoneum between the vagina and rectum is incised, and dissection is deepened to identify the levator ani muscles. Hemostasis is verified, and the sacral promontory is dissected free from surrounding structures, including the lumbosacral trunk and hypogastric nerves. The peritoneal flaps of the rectum are fixated to the sacral promontory with non-absorbable braided interrupted sutures bilaterally.

The surgeon mobilizes the rectum circumferentially, starting posteriorly through the holy plane and progressing towards the pouch of Douglas. The peritoneum between the vagina and rectum is incised, deepening the dissection. The levator ani muscles are identified to define the extent of pelvic mobilization. Hemostasis is verified, and the sacral promontory is dissected free from surrounding structures, including the lumbosacral trunk laterally and hypogastric nerves medially. The peritoneal flaps of the rectum are fixated to the sacral promontory using non-absorbable braided interrupted tuo sutures bilaterally.
The surgeon performs a dissection to the left of the rectum, closing the indicated opening in the peritoneum with non-absorbable running sutures.
The surgeon performs a dissection to the left of the rectum, closing the peritoneal opening with non-absorbable running sutures. The surgical field shows the sutured area indicated by an arrow.

Postoperatively, the patient recovered without complications and was discharged home. On postoperative visits, the patient expressed satisfaction with cosmetic result without recurrence or constipation.
